MC: What is the key difference between clustering and classification?

column3: Clustering requires more training data than classification
Clustering does not use labels during training and has no ground truth to evaluate against

Clustering uses a target column y while classification does not

MC: MC
Why does agglomerative clustering have no predict method?

column3: It was not implemented in scikit-learn due to computational cost
The hierarchical merging process produces assignments only for the points it was given and there is no general rule for placing a new point in the hierarchy
It uses a different import path that does not support predict
Agglomerative clustering always requires the full dataset to be present at prediction time

MC: MC
What does the eps parameter control in DBSCAN?

column3: The minimum number of clusters the algorithm must find
The maximum number of noise points allowed before the algorithm stops
The maximum distance between two points for them to be considered neighbors
The number of times the algorithm restarts with different initializations

MC: MC
Why must data be scaled before applying DBSCAN?

column3: DBSCAN requires all features to be between 0 and 1
DBSCAN uses distance to define neighborhoods and unscaled features with large ranges would dominate the distance calculation, making eps meaningless for smaller-range features
Scaling prevents noise points from being mislabeled as core points
DBSCAN cannot handle negative feature values without scaling

MC: MC
The silhouette score for a clustering result is 0.82. What does this indicate?

column3: The clustering algorithm made errors on 18% of the data points
The clusters overlap by 18%
The algorithm found 82% of the true clusters in the data
Points are on average well-matched to their own cluster and well-separated from other clusters

MC: MC
k-Means produces a higher silhouette score than DBSCAN on a dataset with two crescent-shaped clusters. What is the most likely explanation?

column3: k-Means found the correct clustering and DBSCAN failed on this data
The silhouette score is higher because k-Means used more iterations
DBSCAN labeled too many points as noise, reducing its score
The silhouette score rewards compact blob-shaped clusters, which k-Means always produces regardless of whether they match the actual structure of the data

MC: M
Match each clustering algorithm with its most distinctive characteristic.

column3: KMeans
Requires the number of clusters to be specified and supports predict for new points
AgglomerativeClustering
Starts with every point as its own cluster and merges upward until the target number remains
DBSCAN
Identifies dense regions and labels isolated points as noise without requiring a cluster count

MC: M
Match each DBSCAN term with its definition.

column3: Core point
Has at least min_samples neighbors within distance eps
Boundary point
Within eps of a core point but does not have enough neighbors to be a core point itself
Noise point
Not within eps of any core point and assigned the label -1
eps
The distance threshold that defines the neighborhood around each point

Which of the following are limitations of the silhouette score as a clustering evaluation metric? (Select all that apply)

column3: It measures compactness rather than whether the clusters match the true structure of the data
It systematically favors k-Means because k-Means is designed to produce the compact blob-shaped clusters the metric rewards
